skill.name: controlnet-pose
skill.description: Pose-conditioned generation on RunComfy via the runcomfy CLI. Routes across Kling 2-6 Motion Control Pro / Standard, Wan 2-2 Animate, and Z-Image Turbo ControlNet LoRA. Picks the right route based on video vs still and stylized vs photoreal.
skill.summary: Generate pose-conditioned images and videos using RunComfy CLI with model routing for motion transfer and character animation.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: All 76 static findings are false positives. The skill is a documentation-only markdown file describing RunComfy CLI usage. Backtick patterns are markdown code blocks showing CLI examples. Hardcoded URLs are official RunComfy documentation and catalog links. Filesystem references explain where the CLI stores tokens. No executable code, no malicious intent, and bash is correctly restricted to runcomfy commands only.

content.value_statement: Creating consistent character poses and motion transfers across AI models is complex and error-prone. This skill routes your request to the correct RunComfy model for pose-conditioned image generation or video motion transfer, saving time and reducing trial-and-error.
actual_capabilities: Routes video pose-transfer requests to Kling 2-6 Motion Control Pro or Standard
Routes image pose-conditioned generation to Z-Image Turbo ControlNet LoRA
Routes stylized character animation to Wan 2-2 Animate
Provides CLI invocation examples with correct JSON input schemas
Suggests ComfyUI workflows for multi-condition ControlNet stacks
Explains exit codes and troubleshooting for runcomfy CLI errors
limitations: Requires runcomfy CLI to be installed and authenticated separately
Does not generate pose skeletons or control images upstream
Multi-condition ControlNet stacks require manual ComfyUI workflow use
Only supports bash commands matching runcomfy *

use_cases.title: Transfer motion from a dance video to a character
use_cases.description: A content creator uploads a reference performance video and a target character image. The skill routes to Kling Motion Control Pro to generate a video of the character performing the same dance.

use_cases.title: Generate a pose-locked character illustration
use_cases.description: An artist provides an OpenPose skeleton and a text prompt. The skill routes to Z-Image Turbo ControlNet LoRA to generate an image of the character in the exact specified stance.

use_cases.title: Animate a mascot with audio and pose guidance
use_cases.description: A marketing team wants a stylized mascot to lip-sync and move. The skill routes to Wan 2-2 Animate for audio-driven character animation with pose conditioning.

prompt_templates.title: Basic pose-conditioned image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of a samurai in battle stance using this OpenPose skeleton: https://example.com/pose.png. Use a cherry-blossom forest background and cinematic 35mm style.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner image generation with pose control

prompt_templates.title: Video motion transfer
prompt_templates.prompt: Transfer the motion from this reference video (https://example.com/dance.mp4) onto this character image (https://example.com/character.png). Use Kling Motion Control Pro.

prompt_templates.title: Stylized character animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this stylized mascot image (https://example.com/mascot.png) with this audio file (https://example.com/voice.mp3). Use Wan 2-2 Animate for a cartoon-style result.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-condition ControlNet guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to combine pose + depth + canny edge control for a FLUX generation. Which ComfyUI workflow on RunComfy should I use, and how do I set it up?

output_examples.input: Transfer the motion from this dance video to my character image.
output_examples.output:
I'll route this to Kling 2-6 Motion Control Pro for video pose transfer. Run this command after replacing the URLs with your actual assets:

runcomfy run kling/kling-2-6/motion-control-pro \
  --input '{"reference_video_url": "https://your-cdn.example/dance.mp4", "character_image_url": "https://your-cdn.example/character.png"}' \
  --output-dir ./out

Tips: Use a clean, well-framed reference with a single subject and no scene cuts.

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a warrior in this exact pose.
output_examples.output:
This is best handled by Z-Image Turbo ControlNet LoRA. First generate or obtain an OpenPose skeleton or DWPose image of the desired stance, host it, then run:

runcomfy run tongyi-mai/z-image/turbo/controlnet/lora \
  --input '{"prompt": "A warrior in battle stance, cinematic lighting", "control_image_url": "https://your-cdn.example/pose.png"}' \
  --output-dir ./out

Make sure the control image type matches the LoRA control type.

best_practices: Always use clean, well-framed reference videos with a single subject and no scene cuts for motion transfer
Generate control images upstream with OpenPose or DWPose preprocessors before passing URLs to the CLI
Match the control image type to the LoRA control type specified on the model page
anti_patterns: Do not pipe arbitrary remote install scripts into a shell; use npm or npx to install runcomfy CLI only
Do not use Kling Motion Control for still-image pose conditioning; use Z-Image ControlNet LoRA instead
Do not use photoreal subjects with Wan 2-2 Animate; route those to Kling Motion Control

faq.question: Do I need to install anything before using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. You need the runcomfy CLI installed globally via npm and you must be signed in with runcomfy login or have RUNCOMFY_TOKEN set.

faq.question: Where does the CLI store my API token?
faq.answer: The token is stored in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with file mode 0600. In CI or containers, set the RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable instead.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for multi-condition ControlNet stacks?
faq.answer: The CLI routes in this skill only support single-condition control. For multi-condition stacks like pose plus depth plus canny, you need to use a ComfyUI workflow on RunComfy cloud.

faq.question: What is the difference between Kling Motion Control Pro and Standard?
faq.answer: Pro is the default for final delivery with higher quality. Standard is a cheaper tier best used for drafts and iteration on compositions.

faq.question: Is there a risk of shell injection through prompts or URLs?
faq.answer: No. Prompts and URLs are passed as a JSON string via the --input flag. The CLI does not shell-expand prompt content.

faq.question: What should I do if the output diverges from my prompt?
faq.answer: Suspect the reference asset. Reference videos, character images, and control images are untrusted third-party content. Only ingest URLs the user explicitly provided.
